error_handling.overview: Common error scenarios in UI design work and how to handle them gracefully.

error_scenario.name: token_not_found
error_scenario.description: A component references a CSS variable or Tailwind token that does not exist.

symptoms.symptom: Undefined color or spacing in the browser
Console warning about unknown Tailwind class
Fallback to default browser styling

resolution.step: Check src/tailwind.css for the complete list of defined CSS variables
Verify the token exists in the :root or [data-theme="light"] block
If missing, add it following the established naming convention (--token-name)
Ensure both dark and light mode definitions are present

error_scenario.name: dark_mode_breakage
error_scenario.description: A component renders correctly in dark mode but breaks in light mode or vice versa.

symptoms.symptom: Text invisible against background in one theme
Border or shadow not visible in one theme
Wrong color contrast ratio

resolution.step: Test the component in both data-appearance="dark" and data-theme="light"
Ensure all colors use semantic tokens that adapt between themes
Never use hardcoded oklch values that only work in one theme
Verify src/tailwind.css defines the token for both themes

error_scenario.name: responsive_breakdown
error_scenario.description: A layout breaks or looks incorrect at certain screen sizes.

symptoms.symptom: Content overflow on mobile
Excessive whitespace on tablet
Grid items too narrow or too wide

resolution.step: Test at all breakpoints: mobile (<640px), sm (640px+), md (768px+), lg (1024px+), xl (1280px+)
Use Tailwind responsive prefixes correctly: sm:, md:, lg:, xl:
Apply mobile-first approach (base styles for mobile, prefixes for larger screens)
Use container queries or max-width constraints where appropriate

error_scenario.name: animation_performance
error_scenario.description: Animations cause jank, flickering, or excessive GPU usage.

symptoms.symptom: Dropped frames during animation
LAYOUT or PAINT triggers instead of COMPOSITE only
Animation not respecting prefers-reduced-motion

resolution.step: Animate only transform and opacity for best performance
Avoid animating width, height, top, left, or other layout-triggering properties
Use will-change sparingly and only when needed
Ensure Framer Motion reduced motion mode is enabled globally

symptoms.symptom: No focus visible on interactive elements
Insufficient color contrast ratio
Missing ARIA labels or roles
Screen reader not announcing state changes

resolution.step: Add focus-visible styles using --ring token for all interactive elements
Verify contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text)
Add aria-label, aria-describedby, or role attributes as needed
Use semantic HTML elements (button, nav, main, section) appropriately

error_scenario.name: css_conflict
error_scenario.description: New styles conflict with existing Tailwind or SCSS styles.

symptoms.symptom: Unexpected style applied despite correct classes
Style override chain broken
BEM modifier not applying

resolution.step: Check Tailwind CSS layer ordering (@tailwind base, components, utilities)
Verify no duplicate class names with different values in src/index.scss
Increase specificity only when necessary (avoid !important)
Use cn() from @/lib/utils to merge Tailwind classes safely

error_scenario.name: prop_drilling
error_scenario.description: Component props become unwieldy due to deep nesting.

symptoms.symptom: Component interface has 10+ props
Intermediate components only pass props through
Difficult to track which props affect which UI parts

resolution.step: Extract sub-components with their own interfaces
Use the children pattern for composition
Consider React Context for deeply shared state (theme, cart)
Use className prop for styling overrides instead of boolean flags

recovery_procedure.step: Reproduce the issue in both dark and light modes
Isolate the component causing the issue
Apply the appropriate resolution from the scenarios above
Verify the fix works in both themes and all breakpoints
Update 3_common_patterns.xml if a new reusable pattern emerged from the fix
